$schema-note: Design grammar for the Sage Estate Dashboard. NOT a JSON Schema — a token document consumed by the renderer (layer 6 in plan §2). Authored once (frontend-design pass), reviewed, then revised rarely. Adding a key here for a NEW wing-type / hall / building kind is the estate-design-maintainer trigger (ADR-0002): the agent proposes the new visual grammar as a diff to this file. Volume changes never touch this file.

meta.name: Sage Estate — Nocturne Villa
meta.concept: A Roman villa read at dusk. Cool slate exterior and a dark verdant ground; warm interior light spills from rooms that are active. Each wing-type is a different MATERIAL so the estate reads at a glance before any label loads. Memory that has decayed shows as patina, cracks, and — at the slot-ledger gaps (ADR-0005) — open ruins where a drawer once stood. The whole estate is legible in one still frame; motion only confirms what the eye already grouped. In the 3D Roman renderer (Phase 2c), wing identity is expressed as: tinted wall (65% travertine + 35% wing wall-color for cohesion), colored roof + frieze + window light (full wing palette), and a base-course / pilaster accent strip in the pure wing wall-color (strongest identity cue at LOD 0). No token is voided — every wing reads distinctly against travertine-dominant walls.
meta.direction: refined / atmospheric — not maximalist. Restraint, precise spacing, material honesty. Light does the storytelling.
meta.status: first-draft visual grammar for review per plan §6

grain.note: fine film grain over the whole canvas for depth

ground_grid.note: the iso floor lattice from the locked site-plan diagram

nameplate.use: building + wing nameplates — the inscription on the lintel. Roman capitals.

label.use: room titles, detail-pane prose, glossary terms — a classicist body face.

data.use: counts, ids, sizes, strengths, telemetry — every number and every machine token.

wing_types._note: One entry per value of the wing_type enum (ADR-0004): the 5 registered types + unknown. material + palette + roof + light + motif. The renderer picks by estate-model wing.type; an unmatched type is a hard error (it means sage grew a new wing-type and the design-maintainer must run). 3D Roman renderer identity contract: wall token → tinted wall (65% travertine + 35% wall color) + pilaster (pure wall color); roof → gable roof material; light → window emissive glow. No token is voided.

dev.label: Workshop slate
dev.motif: iron brackets, a worktable in each room
dev.rationale: dev wings are where code is shaped — cool, utilitarian, iron-and-slate.

project.label: Terracotta brick
project.rationale: project wings are lived-in working space — warm brick, the most 'house-like' wing.

knowledge.label: Library travertine
knowledge.motif: tall warm windows, stacked shelving visible at roof-off
knowledge.rationale: knowledge wings = the library — cream stone, warm reading light, the most luminous wing.

ops.label: Gunmetal foundry
ops.motif: vents, gauges, a banked furnace glow
ops.rationale: ops wings are infrastructure — darkest, heaviest, a low green telemetry glow.

meta.label: Verdigris bronze
meta.motif: bronze plaques, a self-referential mirror motif
meta.rationale: meta wings are the framework reflecting on itself — patinated bronze, the rarest material.

personal.label: Private quarters — warm plaster
personal.motif: a hearth alcove, a small lararium niche, personal objects on windowsills
personal.rationale: personal wings are the user's own memory space — warm, domestic, distinct from project (fired brick) by being softer/lighter, closer to inhabited plaster than industrial clay.

unknown.label: Unfinished ruin
unknown.motif: bare scaffold poles, a chalked '?' on the wall, no door
unknown.rationale: the '?' bucket made visible (ADR-0004). A malformed/unregistered wing is a roofless ruin the eye is meant to flag. Never hidden — parity with dashboard.py.

nook_palace.label: The Nook — Palace
nook_palace.grandeur: highest — the jewel of the estate; footprint scales with wing count
nook_palace.rationale: memory is identity — the Palace is the largest, most-lit, most-detailed structure.

workshop.label: The Workshop (Fabrica)
workshop.rationale: where the agents work — gold forge-light, one lit room per active agent.

grounds.label: The Grounds (Peristylium)
grounds.rationale: an open colonnaded courtyard, NOT a building — repo plots tile to fit; grows without bound.

gate.label: The Gate — danger zone
gate.motif: the single guarded door out; ember glow

state_signals._note: How estate-model fields become visible state. These map to schema fields directly so the renderer is data-driven, not hand-flagged.

active_agent.visual: room window lit warm (palette.accent.gold), soft pulse

dirty_repo.visual: scaffolding + sawhorses on the plot, dust haze

decayed_memory.visual: closet face fades + hairline cracks; opacity = lerp(strength, floor..1)

ruin_gap.trigger: slot-ledger gap — an id deleted, slot never reclaimed (ADR-0005)
ruin_gap.visual: open foundation / broken closet where a drawer once stood; never repacked

danger_zone.visual: the Gate glows red (signal.danger), ember particles

zoom._note: 4-level semantic zoom (plan §7). LOD thresholds are camera-distance bands; cross a band → reveal/hide a layer. Roof-lift is the signature transition.

levels.name: Estate
levels.shows: all buildings + grounds, roofs ON, nameplates only

levels.name: Roof off
levels.shows: enter a building → roof lifts → wings/zones + rooms inside

levels.name: Closer
levels.shows: inside a room → closets group its contents

levels.name: Closest
levels.shows: drawers (Nook) / agent.md (Workshop) / config settings (Tablinum) — KEEP THESE LEAF TYPES DISTINCT, do not flatten all to 'drawers'

zoom.leaf_distinction_note: Locked vocabulary (HANDOFF §2): a drawer is a verbatim MEMORY unit only. At level 3 the Nook's leaf is a drawer, the Workshop's leaf is an agent.md file, the Tablinum's leaf is a config setting. The renderer + detail pane MUST label them by their true type — flattening all leaves to 'drawer' is a vocabulary regression.

roof_lift.note: roof translates up + fades; interior staggers in 40ms per zone

camera_fly.note: detail-pane select flies camera to target; two-way

detail_pane.mirrors: current selection — wing stats / room drawers / agent.md / drawer metadata (body fetched lazily on open, loopback only — ADR-0003)

accessibility.min_contrast: WCAG AA on all text over its panel
accessibility.non_color_state: every state_signal carries a NON-color cue (cracks, scaffold, ruin geometry, icon) — never color alone, for color-blind legibility
accessibility.motion: respect prefers-reduced-motion: roof-lift + fly become instant cuts; pulses disabled
